content.text:
Looking at the image, the track curves to the right ahead with the dashed center line visible bending rightward, and the car appears to be slightly left of center but on course following the curve.

**Waypoint Analysis:**
- nearest_waypoints[1] = 83, target = (83 + 5) % 121 = 88
- Target WP88: x=0.8051, y=4.0066
- Car: x=1.1693, y=4.2272, heading=-148.14°
- dy = 4.0066 - 4.2272 = -0.2206
- dx = 0.8051 - 1.1693 = -0.3642
- bearing = atan2(-0.2206, -0.3642) → third quadrant
- angle = atan(0.2206/0.3642) = atan(0.6057) = 31.17°
- bearing = -(180° - 31.17°) = -148.83°
- delta = -148.83° - (-148.14°) = -0.69°
- |delta| < 5°: nearly perfectly aligned, steer 0°

```json
{
  "reasoning": "Delta of -0.69° to WP88 means the car is nearly perfectly aligned with the upcoming waypoint on this rightward curve, so I maintain speed with zero steering to avoid oscillation.",
  "knowledge": [
    "Positive steering = left turn, negative = right turn",
    "Delta under 5° means steer 0 to avoid oscillation",
    "Delta 5-15° use ±5°; 15-30° use ±10 to ±15°; 30-50° use ±15 to ±20°; >50° use ±25°",
    "Near WP80-90, track curves right — expect small negative delta values requiring minimal steering",
    "Speed 0.75 for |delta|>50°; 1.0 for 30-50°; 1.5 for <30°",
    "Car is slightly left of center (~0.127m) — consistent with right curve approach",
    "Heading near -148° corresponds to southwest travel on this section of track",
    "Track curves sharply right after WP83 toward WP88-90 — watch for increasing delta",
    "Zero steering when delta < 5° prevents oscillation and maintains max speed",
    "Track width ~0.76m — small corrections are sufficient, avoid overcorrecting"
  ],
  "movement_analysis": {
    "x": [1.423, 1.335, 1.263, 1.169],
    "y": [4.407, 4.337, 4.283, 4.227],
    "yaw": [-153.48, -148.95, -147.58, -148.14]
  },
  "waypoint_analysis": "Target WP88 at (0.805, 4.007): dy=-0.220, dx=-0.364, bearing=-148.83°, delta=-0.69° → nearly aligned, steer 0°",
  "speed": 1.5,
  "steering_angle": 0.0
}
```
